env.c(3) Library Functions Manual env.c(3)

NAME

env.c - Environment variable utilities with stack-based prompt responses.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


bool env_pop_prompt_response (char *response_out, size_t response_size)
Pop the first response from ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE stack. bool env_has_prompt_response (void)
Check if ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE has any responses available. bool env_validate_prompt_response_format (void)
Validate ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE format.

Detailed Description

Environment variable utilities with stack-based prompt responses.

Definition in file env.c.

Function Documentation

bool env_has_prompt_response (void )

Check if ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE has any responses available.

Returns

true if at least one response is available, false otherwise

Definition at line 201 of file env.c.

201                                    {
202   const char *env_value = SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
203   if (!env_value || env_value[0] == '\0' || env_value[0] == ';') {
204     return false;
205   }
206 
207   // Check if it's all semicolons
208   for (const char *p = env_value; *p; p++) {
209     if (*p != ';') {
210       return true;
211     }
212   }
213 
214   return false;
215 }




References SAFE_GETENV.






bool env_pop_prompt_response (char * response_out, size_t
  response_size)


Pop the first response from ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE
    stack. Format: "response1;response2;response3" or
    "response1;response2;response3;" This function extracts the first
    response and updates the environment variable to contain the remaining
    responses.




Escaping: Use backslash to include literal semicolons or
    backslashes:


    
  
  • \; → ; (literal semicolon)
    • 
  
  • \ → \ (literal backslash)
    • 



Examples:


    
  
  • "y;n;123" -> returns "y", sets env to
      "n;123"
    • 
  
  • "password" -> returns "password", sets env to
      ""
    • 
  
  • "y;" -> returns "y", sets env to ""
    • 
  
  • "pass\;word;y" -> returns "pass;word", sets env to
      "y"
    • 
  
  • "path\\to\\file;n" -> returns "path\to\file", sets
      env to "n"
    • 
  
  • "" -> returns NULL
    • 
  
  • ";;y" -> returns NULL (invalid format)
    • 
  
  • ";y" -> returns NULL (invalid format)
    • 



Parameters


response_out Buffer to store the popped response
  (must be at least 256 bytes)


response_size Size of response_out buffer


Returns


true if a response was successfully popped, false
  otherwise


Definition at line 93 of file env.c.


93                                                                        {
94   if (!response_out || response_size < 2) {
95     return false;
96   }
97 
98   const char *env_value = SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
99   if (!env_value || env_value[0] == '\0') {
100     return false;
101   }
102 
103   // Validate: no leading semicolon (unless escaped)
104   if (env_value[0] == ';') {
105     log_warn("Invalid ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE format: leading semicolon");
106     return false;
107   }
108 
109   // Find the first unescaped semicolon
110   const char *semicolon = find_unescaped_semicolon(env_value);
111 
112   if (semicolon == NULL) {
113     // No semicolon - this is the last (or only) response
114     size_t len = strlen(env_value);
115     if (len == 0) {
116       return false;
117     }
118 
119     // Validate: ensure it's not empty
120     if (len >= response_size) {
121       log_warn("Response too long: %zu bytes (max: %zu)", len, response_size - 1);
122       return false;
123     }
124 
125     // Copy the response
126     SAFE_STRNCPY(response_out, env_value, response_size);
127 
128     // Unescape the response (\; → ; and \\ → \)
129     unescape_response(response_out);
130 
131     // Clear the environment variable
132     platform_unsetenv("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
133 
134     log_dev("Popped last response from stack: '%s' (cleared env)", response_out);
135     return true;
136   }
137 
138   // Calculate response length
139   size_t response_len = (size_t)(semicolon - env_value);
140 
141   // Validate: no empty segment
142   if (response_len == 0) {
143     log_warn("Invalid ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE format: empty segment");
144     return false;
145   }
146 
147   if (response_len >= response_size) {
148     log_warn("Response too long: %zu bytes (max: %zu)", response_len, response_size - 1);
149     return false;
150   }
151 
152   // Copy the first response
153   memcpy(response_out, env_value, response_len);
154   response_out[response_len] = '\0';
155 
156   // Unescape the response (\; → ; and \\ → \)
157   unescape_response(response_out);
158 
159   // Calculate remaining stack (skip the semicolon)
160   const char *remaining = semicolon + 1;
161 
162   // Validate: if remaining is empty or only semicolons, clear the env
163   bool remaining_valid = false;
164   for (const char *p = remaining; *p; p++) {
165     if (*p != ';') {
166       remaining_valid = true;
167       break;
168     }
169   }
170 
171   // Update environment variable with remaining responses
172   if (remaining_valid && remaining[0] != '\0') {
173     char new_env[1024];
174     int written = snprintf(new_env, sizeof(new_env), "ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE=%s", remaining);
175     if (written < 0 || (size_t)written >= sizeof(new_env)) {
176       log_warn("Failed to format new environment variable");
177       return false;
178     }
179 
180     if (platform_setenv("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE", remaining) != 0) {
181       log_warn("Failed to update ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
182       return false;
183     }
184 
185     log_debug("Popped response from stack: '%s' (remaining: '%s')", response_out, remaining);
186   } else {
187     // No valid remaining responses - clear the env
188     platform_unsetenv("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
189 
190     log_debug("Popped response from stack: '%s' (cleared env)", response_out);
191   }
192 
193   return true;
194 }






References log_debug, log_dev, log_warn,
    platform_setenv(), platform_unsetenv(), SAFE_GETENV,
    and SAFE_STRNCPY.






bool env_validate_prompt_response_format (void )


Validate ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE format. Valid
    formats:


    
  
  • "y"
    • 
  
  • "y;n"
    • 
  
  • "y;n;123"
    • 
  
  • "y;n;123;"
    • 
  
  • "pass\;word;y" (escaped semicolon)
    • 
  
  • "path\\to\\file" (escaped backslash)
    • 



Invalid formats:


    
  
  • ""
    • 
  
  • ";"
    • 
  
  • ";y"
    • 
  
  • "y;;n" (empty segment)
    • 
  
  • ";;"
    • 



Returns


true if format is valid, false otherwise


Definition at line 237 of file env.c.


237                                                {
238   const char *env_value = SAFE_GETENV("ASCII_CHAT_QUESTION_PROMPT_RESPONSE");
239   if (!env_value) {
240     return true; // Not set is valid (no automated responses)
241   }
242 
243   if (env_value[0] == '\0') {
244     return true; // Empty is valid (no automated responses)
245   }
246 
247   // Invalid: leading semicolon (unless escaped)
248   if (env_value[0] == ';') {
249     return false;
250   }
251 
252   // Check for consecutive unescaped semicolons or empty segments
253   const char *p = env_value;
254   bool last_was_semicolon = false;
255 
256   while (*p) {
257     if (*p == '\\' && *(p + 1)) {
258       // Skip escaped character
259       last_was_semicolon = false;
260       p += 2;
261       continue;
262     }
263     if (*p == ';') {
264       if (last_was_semicolon) {
265         return false; // Consecutive unescaped semicolons (empty segment)
266       }
267       last_was_semicolon = true;
268     } else {
269       last_was_semicolon = false;
270     }
271     p++;
272   }
273 
274   // Trailing semicolon is allowed
275   return true;
276 }






References SAFE_GETENV.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>

    ascii-chat
